compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
# Remediate validated findings from the behavioral/contractual test-coverage audit

An external test-coverage audit (static, pinned to commit `2d1524ad8`) identified a
cluster of tests that assert against **source text / private structure / mere
existence** rather than behavior ("change-detector" tests), plus several documented
contracts with **no behavioral regression test**, and two documentation-vs-code
drifts. We re-verified every finding against the actual codebase with five
independent review passes: **all findings validated** (two downgraded to
"partial — the mechanism is partly tested but the specific gap is real"), and the
grep-against-source inventory surfaced **additional** anti-pattern tests beyond the
report's list.

This epic converts the false-confidence regressions into real behavioral coverage,
adds the missing data-safety/concurrency tests, hardens two thin production paths the
audit exposed, and reconciles the stale docs.

## Context

- The audit's core thesis is correct: the largest residual risk is **not** broad
  lack of testing — core coverage (CRUD parity, deterministic replay, two-clone
  reconvergence, no-loss writer storms, claim contention, detector verdict
  semantics) is strong — but **false confidence** in narrowly-targeted regressions
  whose tests inspect source structure instead of exercising the promised behavior.
- Verification method: five parallel read-only audit passes over production source,
  existing tests, docs, and CI config. Findings that were already covered were
  dropped; only genuinely-missing/miswired coverage is ticketed here.
- Grep-against-source is **not** universally an anti-pattern here: 59 of 77
  source-inspecting tests are JUSTIFIED (the source text *is* the observable
  contract — exact git argv an adapter builds, layering/import invariants via
  `ast.parse`, prompt/YAML/Terraform/CI "config-as-artifact" drift guards,
  runtime-produced artifacts). This epic only touches the ~9 ANTI-PATTERN and the
  handful of BORDERLINE cases where a behavioral test is feasible and stronger.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Every P0 finding (index.lock stale-reclaim TOCTOU; detached-HEAD local-ahead
      preservation) has a deterministic behavioral regression test, and the
      production path is hardened where the race is real.
- [ ] Each "change-detector" regression test named by the audit is either replaced
      by a behavioral test that fails when the behavior breaks, deleted as redundant
      (a behavioral sibling already covers it), or explicitly re-justified as a
      supplemental structural guard — never left counted as behavioral coverage.
- [ ] The two documentation drifts (Jira link-sync inventory; dual-lock portability
      lifecycle) are reconciled so contract-to-test traceability is trustworthy.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` stays green; no net loss of
      meaningful coverage.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All child stories are closed (signed), each landing through the Gerrit gate
      (LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1).
- [ ] No test in the changed set asserts only `hasattr(...)`, an exact private
      constant value, or a source substring where the underlying behavior is
      executable and was previously untested.
- [ ] Newly-added concurrency/race tests are deterministic (barrier/instrumented
      seams, not sleep-timing races that can pass on a slow runner).

file_impact.reason: index.lock reclaim TOCTOU + run_git wrapper hardening/tests
fcntl errno discrimination, canonical_tracker, dual_window lifecycle
change-detector tests replaced with behavioral coverage across suite

compiled_state.title: Remediate validated findings from the test-coverage audit
